ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn entry FOODON_03302649 represents churro as a food item; this record is a commercial multi-piece box serving with dipping sauces. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers fried dough products; churros are a specific Spanish fried dough pastry. Closest parent used as no churro-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for churros or fried dough pastry at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 box (12 cups × 5 churros each = 60 churros, with 12 dipping sauces)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Classic Big Box contains 60 warm churros arranged in 12 cups of 5 pieces each, accompanied by 12 individual dipping sauce portions; a realistic individual serving is approximately 5 churros (75 g) with one sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough pastry) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for plain churros (fried dough) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent the churro dough component per 100 g. Dipping sauce contribution is noted separately; the per_100g values here reflect the churro pastry itself. Sauce macros would increase sugar and fat if included proportionally, but the primary food item is the churro dough.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous reference: fried dough / doughnut GI data from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy with plain fried dough products (doughnuts, fried pastry) which range GI 74–76 in the Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables. Churros share a similar wheat-flour deep-fried dough matrix. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (74 × 38.0) / 100 ≈ 28.1, based on a 75 g individual serving containing approximately 38 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high energy density
hypertension.guidance: Each 75 g individual serving of churros contributes approximately 210 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and be mindful of cumulative sodium from dipping sauces, which may add a further 50–150 mg sodium per sauce portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 74) and high glycemic load per serving (GL ~28)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Churros are a high-GI, high-GL fried dough pastry; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence, limit to a small portion (1–2 pieces), pair with a protein or fat source to blunt postprandial glucose response, and account for the additional sugar load from dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, and energy density from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Churros are deep-fried in oil, providing approximately 19.8 g fat and 4.2 g saturated fat per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to small occasional portions and choose dipping sauces with lower saturated fat content to reduce overall cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Classic Churros Big Box (60 pieces) is a Spanish-origin fried dough pastry dessert sold as a party or sharing box by Churros Republic at Funan Mall in Singapore via field survey. The box contains 60 warm classic churros arranged in 12 individual cups of 5 pieces each, accompanied by 12 individual dipping sauce portions. Churros are made from a simple choux-style dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and oil, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crisp on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior. They are typically dusted with cinnamon sugar and served warm. The dipping sauces commonly include chocolate, caramel, or strawberry variants. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense (approximately 398 kcal per 100 g), high in refined carbohydrates (51 g per 100 g), and moderately high in fat (19.8 g per 100 g) due to deep-frying. The glycemic index is estimated at 74 (high), making this a high-glycemic-load food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular risk. The sodium content of approximately 280 mg per 100 g is relevant for individuals with hypertension. This product is suitable for vegetarians and is likely halal-certified given the restaurant context in Singapore. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance or wheat allergy.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of churros?
How many churros in the big box?
Classic churros with chocolate sauce nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for plain fried churros, which is the closest approved source match. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for churros. GI is an analogous estimate from fried dough products in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact product-level nutrient analysis is available for this specific commercial product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the churro pastry component per 100 g, adapted from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 175939). Dipping sauce macros are not included in per_100g values as sauce composition varies by flavour. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for churros. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — fried dough pastries contain no vitamin C. 10. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate based on fried dough products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct churros GI measurement was found. 11. Serving size of 75 g represents one cup of 5 churros as described in the product listing. 12. The food_name has been normalised from the menu name 'Classic Big Box 60' to 'Classic Churros Big Box (60 pieces)' for canonical clarity. 13. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore field survey listing. 14. Halal status is assumed likely given Singapore food service context but not confirmed from an official halal certification body.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name normalised to canonical English 'Classic Churros Big Box (60 pieces)'. Multilingual names present for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for a Singapore-served food item.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 398.0, protein_g 4.9, fat_g 19.8, carbohydrate_g 51.0) are present, numeric, and sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175939 for plain churros. nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.5), sodium_mg (280.0), saturated_fat_g (4.2), and sugar_g (8.5) are all present as numeric values consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for fried dough pastry.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 74 (numeric, >0), gi_category 'high', glycemic_load_per_serving 28.1, gi_source fully documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database fried dough data, clearly documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03302649 (churro) used as closest_parent. FoodEx2 A0EJX (fried dough products) used as closest_parent. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes invented. All match_type values are from the allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none).

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries and meal_context are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (high), Cardiovascular Disease (high). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 19 entries covering English variants, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully consistent with flat aliases list — all strings in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all 6 hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Nutrient data sourced from USDA SR Legacy for plain churros (adapted match). GI estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database fried dough data. Multilingual names provided for Singapore context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). All sentinel -1 values documented. Aliases consistent between flat list and aliases_by_language. Ontology uses only allowed match_type values. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance.
